growth_records.medium: Obligate intracellular growth in Cos-7 mammalian host cell monolayers (no defined bacterial growth medium)
growth_records.cite: Chiarelli et al. Single-Inclusion Kinetics of Chlamydia trachomatis Development. mSystems (2020). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00689-20. [PMC7567582]
growth_records.snippet: Simulations of the two models ... using a multiplicity of infection (MOI) of 1 and an RB generation time of 2.27 h produced results that mimic the general kinetics of the chlamydial cycle ... 1.13 h (half the measured RB doubling time) ...
growth_records.notes: Measured RB (reticulate body) doubling/generation time = 2.27 h at 37°C (the paper labels 1.13 h and 4.54 h as half and 2x the 'measured RB doubling time', confirming 2.27 h is the measured value). Growth rate calculated: μ=ln2/td=0.693/2.27=0.305 h−1. C. trachomatis is an obligate intracellular pathogen grown in Cos-7 host cells, so there is no defined bacterial medium and no measured substrate uptake/secretion fluxes. Slower/faster replication observed at 35°C/40°C respectively but no numeric doubling times reported for those temperatures.
